title: Decompose figures to find volume
description: Find the volume of irregular 3D figures by dividing the figures into rectangular prisms and finding the volume of each part.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  

[[☃ image 3]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 5 \times 9 \times2 = \blueE{90} \text{ cubic cm}$
 

[[☃ image 4]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=1\times 9\times 8 = \purpleE{72} \text{ cubic cm}$ 

 Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \blueE{90} + \purpleE{72} = \tealE{162} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\tealE{162} \text{ cubic centimeters}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 5 centimeters length by 9 centimeters width, and has a height of 2 centimeters. The second rectangular prism is next to the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 1 centimeter length by 9 centimeters width and has a height of 8 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

  
$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 5 \times 4 \times3 = \blueE{60} \text{ cubic cm}$


[[☃ image 2]]

  
$\text{Volume of prism 2}=2 \times 4\times 8 = \purpleE{64} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \blueE{60} + \purpleE{64} = \tealE{124} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\tealE{124} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 5 centimeters length by 4 centimeters width, and has a height of 3 centimeters. The second rectangular prism sits next to the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 2 centimeters length by 4 centimeters width and has a height of 8 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

   
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 2\times 6 \times5 = \goldE{60} \text{ cubic in}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=8\times 1\times 5 = \purpleE{40} \text{ cubic in}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \goldE{60} + \purpleE{40} = \blueE{100} \text{ cubic in}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{100} \text{ cubic inches}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 2 inches length by 6 inches width, and has a height of 5 inches. The second rectangular prism sits behind the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 8 inches length by 1 inch width and has a height of 5 inches.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 6 \times 7 \times1 = \purpleE{42} \text{ cubic cm}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=3 \times 7\times 4 = \goldE{84} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \purpleE{42} + \goldE{84} = \blueE{126} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{126} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 6 centimeters length by 7 centimeters width, and has a height of 1 centimeter. The second rectangular prism sits next to the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 3 centimeters length by 7 centimeters width and has a height of 4 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 3\times 5 \times 7= \goldE{105} \text{ cubic cm}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=3\times 5\times 2 = \purpleD{30} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = \goldE{105} + \purpleD{30} = \maroonD{135} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\maroonD{135} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 3 centimeters length by 5 centimeters width, and has a height of 7 centimeters. The second rectangular prism sits in front of the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 3 centimeters length by 5 centimeters width and has a height of 2 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 9 \times 10 \times2 = \purpleE{180} \text{ cubic cm}$



[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=3 \times 10\times 5 = \blueE{150} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = \purpleE{180} + \blueE{150} = \goldE{330} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\goldE{330} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. The first rectangular prism has a base that measures 9 centimeters length by 10 centimeters width, and has a height of 2 centimeters. The second rectangular prism sits next to the first rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 3 centimeters length by 10 centimeters width and has a height of 5 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

  
$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 7\times 8 \times1 = \goldE{56} \text{ cubic in}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=10\times 9\times 1 = \blueE{90} \text{ cubic in}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \goldE{56} + \blueE{90} = \tealE{146} \text{ cubic in}$
The volume of the figure is $\tealE{146} \text{ cubic inches}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. One prism is gold and the other is blue. The gold rectangular prism has a base that measures 7 inches length by 8 inches width, and has a height of 1 inch. The blue rectangular prism sits in behind the green rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 10 inches length by 9 inches width and has a height of 1 inch.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 2\times 8 \times2 = \purpleE{32} \text{ cubic cm}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=3 \times 8\times 6 = \goldE{144} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = \purpleE{32} + \goldE{144} = \blueE{176} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{176} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. One prism is purple and the other is gold. The purple rectangular prism has a base that measures 2 centimeters length by 8 centimeters width, and has a height of 2 centimeters. The gold rectangular prism sits next to the purple rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 3 centimeters length by 8 centimeters width and has a height of 6 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangular prisms.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of this figure?**    

 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 10 \times 6 \times3 = \blueE{180} \text{ cubic cm}$

[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=4 \times 6\times 4 = \maroonE{96} \text{ cubic cm}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = \blueE{180} + \maroonE{96} = \tealE{276} \text{ cubic cm}$
The volume of the figure is $\tealE{276} \text{ cubic cm}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. Colors blue and maroon are used to tell them apart. The blue rectangular prism has a base that measures 10 centimeters length by 6 centimeters width, and has a height of 3 centimeters. The maroon rectangular prism sits next to the blue rectangular prism. It has a base that measures 4 centimeters length by 6 centimeters width and has a height of 4 centimeters.

hints:
We can think of this figure as $2$ rectangular prisms that are stuck together.  Let's find the volume of each prism separately.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1} = 5 \times 7 \times1 = \blueE{35} \text{ cubic in}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=9 \times 7\times 4 = \maroonD{252} \text{ cubic in}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = \blueE{35} + \maroonD{252} = \purpleE{287} \text{ cubic in}$
The volume of the figure is $\purpleE{287} \text{ cubic inches}$.

options.alt: A shape made up of two rectangular prisms. Colors are used to set them apart. It has a base that measures 9 inches length by 7 inches width and has a height of 4 inches.

hints:

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 9\times 6 \times3 = \purpleE{162} \text{ cubic in}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=10\times 7\times 3 = \blueE{210} \text{ cubic in}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.

hints:

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 5\times 4 \times 5= \blueE{100} \text{ cubic m}$


[[☃ image 2]]

$\text{Volume of prism 2}=2\times 9\times 5 = \purpleD{90} \text{ cubic m}$

Now, we add to find the volume of the entire figure.
